name: San Juan County Fry Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil to 375 degrees in a deep skillet.
2. Combine flour, salt, baking powder, and water.
3. Mix well. Add more flour until the dough is soft.
4. Turn out onto a floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic.
5. Pinch off about a 3-inch ball of dough.
6. Flatten the dough with hands and stretch into a 7-8 inch circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
7. Fry in hot oil until light golden brown on both sides.
8. Drain on paper towels.
9. Serve with stew (mutton is best), jam/jelly, or with honey and butter.
10. This bread pairs well with Navajo Tacos.
This recipe makes between 8-12 servings.
